reasoning: The informal claim is "X^2+1 is irreducible in F_7". The Lean encoding quantifies over any field F with a Fintype instance and card F = 7, and asserts Irreducible (X^2+1) in Polynomial F. By the classification of finite fields, any such F is (uniquely up to isomorphism) the field ZMod 7 = F_7, and ring isomorphism preserves irreducibility of polynomials. Hence "for all fields F with |F|=7, X^2+1 is irreducible in F[X]" is logically equivalent to "X^2+1 is irreducible in F_7". The conclusion uses Polynomial.Irreducible correctly on X^2+1. No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what is needed to specify "the" field of order 7.
